reset_biology_synthesis.recommendation: Reset Biology suggests asking the practitioner to consider Hunter's 5-10 mg/day oral chronic frame as the operative starting range, since Hunter (4igc2UpFr7Q 00:34:16.240): "then for spermidine, I like somewhere around like 10 mg per day of spermidine" and Hunter email threadId 19c970e1745b6a39 (2026-02-25): "Hunter's sweet spot 5-10 mg per day - Above dietary intake - Well within studied safety margins." We'd ask the prescribing physician to weigh whether typical store-bought wheat-germ products even reach that floor, since Hunter email threadId 19cfdd3f61ffe05e (2026-03-17): "Typical wheat germ / plant extract supplement: 0.6-1.2 mg/day - Probably TOO LOW to move the needle." Taylor and Trevor have no spermidine content in the corpus, so the recommendation rests entirely on Hunter; treat this as a longevity/autophagy background player rather than an acute-injury tool.
reset_biology_synthesis.caveats:
- Single-expert sourcing: only Hunter has documented spermidine content; Taylor (women-focused) and Trevor (SmashweRx) have zero mentions in the corpus, so cross-validation is not available.
- B12 depletion at higher doses: Hunter email threadId 19cfdd3f61ffe05e (2026-03-17): "B12 depletion at higher doses (similar to metformin) -> just supplement B12."
- Minor joint/muscle issues at higher doses: Hunter email threadId 19cfdd3f61ffe05e (2026-03-17): "Some minor joint + muscle issues at higher doses."
- Hypertrophy goal mismatch: Hunter email threadId 19cfdd3f61ffe05e (2026-03-17): "If your only goal right now is putting on as much muscle as possible, maybe hold off."
- Redundancy with other autophagy stacks: Hunter email threadId 19c970e1745b6a39 (2026-02-25): "If you're already running rapamycin, aggressive fasting, or strong autophagy stacks -> may be redundant."
- Dose ceiling reference: Hunter email threadId 19cfdd3f61ffe05e (2026-03-17): "Highest dose tested: 40 mg/day for 28 days, ZERO adverse events in healthy older men" and Hunter (4igc2UpFr7Q 00:34:19.600): "You can go as high as 40 mg." Hunter email threadId 19cfdd3f61ffe05e (2026-03-17): "Hunter wouldn't go that high himself."
- Not an NAD+ precursor: Hunter email threadId 19c970e1745b6a39 (2026-02-25): "Spermidine does NOT raise NAD+ levels directly + is NOT a precursor - BUT improves NAD+ efficiency."
- 12-month memory trial null result was dose-related: Hunter email threadId 19cfdd3f61ffe05e (2026-03-17): "Larger 12-month trial at only 0.9 mg/day: NO significant memory benefit -> Dose was too low - blood spermidine levels didn't change."
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_consensus: Cross-expert consensus is not available for spermidine: only Hunter has documented spermidine content in the Reset Biology corpus. Hunter's own internal consistency across two emails and one podcast: Hunter email threadId 19c970e1745b6a39 (2026-02-25): "Hunter's sweet spot 5-10 mg per day"; Hunter email threadId 19cfdd3f61ffe05e (2026-03-17): "Hunter's sweet spot 5-10 mg/day"; Hunter (4igc2UpFr7Q 00:34:16.240): "then for spermidine, I like somewhere around like 10 mg per day of spermidine." Mechanism framing is consistent across emails: Hunter email threadId 19c970e1745b6a39 (2026-02-25): "Primary action: induces autophagy" and "works via EP300 acetyltransferase inhibition"; Hunter email threadId 19cfdd3f61ffe05e (2026-03-17): "Spermidine triggers it by blocking EP300 (protein that normally puts brakes on cleanup)."
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_disagreement: No cross-expert disagreement is documentable: Taylor and Trevor have no spermidine content in the corpus. Within Hunter's own material there is a tension between studied dose ranges and his personal sweet spot, captured verbatim: Hunter email threadId 19c970e1745b6a39 (2026-02-25): "Plant-extract studies: 1-1.2 mg/day" vs "Safety in healthy older adults: 40 mg/day (good tolerability)" vs "PK studies: 15 mg/day" vs "Hunter's sweet spot 5-10 mg per day." There is also a hedge against high-end dosing: Hunter (4igc2UpFr7Q 00:34:19.600): "You can go as high as 40 mg" but Hunter email threadId 19cfdd3f61ffe05e (2026-03-17): "Hunter wouldn't go that high himself."

chronic.dose: Hunter (4igc2UpFr7Q 00:34:16.240): "then for spermidine, I like somewhere around like 10 mg per day of spermidine"; Hunter email threadId 19c970e1745b6a39 (2026-02-25): "Hunter's sweet spot 5-10 mg per day." Hunter (4igc2UpFr7Q 00:34:19.600) hedges higher: "You can go as high as 40 mg."
chronic.route: Oral. Hunter email threadId 19cfdd3f61ffe05e (2026-03-17): "NAD Regen by BioStack Labs - biostacklabs.com/hunter - 5 mg spermidine per serving" (oral capsule context, no injectable route taught).
chronic.frequency: Daily. Hunter (4igc2UpFr7Q 00:34:16.240): "10 mg per day of spermidine."
chronic.duration: Continuous, not pulsed. Hunter email threadId 19c970e1745b6a39 (2026-02-25): "Cycling philosophy - 'Not something I would pulse' - View as consistent background longevity signal - mitochondrial maintenance, not acute intervention."
chronic.verbatim_citation: Hunter (4igc2UpFr7Q 00:34:16.240): "then for spermidine, I like somewhere around like 10 mg per day of spermidine." Hunter email threadId 19c970e1745b6a39 (2026-02-25): "Hunter's sweet spot 5-10 mg per day - Above dietary intake - Well within studied safety margins - Likely meaningful autophagy signaling without experimental megadosing." Hunter email threadId 19cfdd3f61ffe05e (2026-03-17): "Hunter's sweet spot 5-10 mg/day - Where Hunter personally notices difference in energy + mental clarity."
